Skip to contents
MixedUpParents 0.1.0.9999
  • Reference
    • Example run of MixedUpParents

Calculate the genotype probabilities for pairs of individuals

pairwise_genotype_probs.Rd

More description later

Usage

pairwise_genotype_probs(L, kid, par, debug = 0L)

Arguments

L

the list output from prepare_for_gpp().

kid

the ID of the individual (the actual ID, not the iIdx) to be treated as the kid.

par

a vector of the actual IDs of the individuals to be treated as parents

debug

an integer flag. debug1 = 0 means return no debug information. Otherwise the function returns a list with a lot of extra information that can be used to verify that the calculations are being done correctly. If debug = 1, then information is returned at the rate of one row per segment, and no genotype information gets returned. If debug = 2, then information is returned at one row per marker, and it includes the genotype probabilities for each marker in the unrelated case. If debug = 3 then information is returned with one row for each marker and each distinct value of the ancestry of the haplotype segregated from the parent. (This allows us to check the calculations for the probability of genotypes given the ancestries and the segregated haplotype). NOTE: debug > 1 runs pretty slowly because there are so many push_back calls. If you are using these, it would be best to pass it farly small data sets (i.e. one candidate kid compare to only a handful of candidate parents).

On this page
